    "R1.1": "The revised introduction clearly identifies the load-bearing concept (QuerySieve) and shows where the draft drifts from it (background discussion). The reframing survives prior-work contrast by naming Zheng et al. and Dubois et al. as related work.",
    "R1.4": "The revised introduction names one prior work (Zheng et al.) and articulates the delta: QuerySieve addresses length bias in LLM judges, while Zheng et al. address length bias in MT-Bench.",

    "RX.3": "The revised introduction covers exactly the agreed J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original (length bias) and proposes a solution.",

rationale.R1.1: The revised introduction clearly identifies the load-bearing concept (QuerySieve) and shows where the draft drifts from it (background discussion). The reframing survives prior-work contrast by naming Zheng et al. and Dubois et al. as related work.
rationale.R1.4: The revised introduction names one prior work (Zheng et al.) and articulates the delta: QuerySieve addresses length bias in LLM judges, while Zheng et al. address length bias in MT-Bench.
rationale.RX.1: The output does not mark confidence or missing evidence for any recommendation.
rationale.RX.3: The revised introduction covers exactly the agreed J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original (length bias) and proposes a solution.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason, but major rewrites are traced.
